steps.title: Read the broad claim
steps.content: The underlined third sentence says that evolutionary links between predators and prey can persist across centuries and continents. That is a general claim about predator-prey relationships, not a detail limited to one species, one location, or one research team.

steps.title: Track the example around it
steps.content: The surrounding sentences provide the specific example. Mimosa trees evolved in East Asia with B. terrenus, were moved to North America in 1785 without the beetle, and were attacked quickly when the beetle later appeared near monitored trees. The mimosa and beetle story illustrates the broad claim that the predator-prey link persisted despite time and distance.

steps.title: Match the function
steps.content: Choice A invents a planned hypothesis by Tran's team. Choice C says the sentence explains why species spread, which it does not. Choice D calls it an alternative explanation, but it functions as the principle illustrated by the later example. Choice B describes that structure accurately. The answer is B.
